source.description: A reviewed six-panel raster contact sheet showing one set of track starting blocks from pale perspective construction to a finished graphite and blue-pencil sketch.
source.notes: The same three-quarter view, two footplates, central rail, and lane placement must persist across all six panels.

process_strategy: Build the long central rail first, attach both angled footplates to reserved bracket positions, then add hardware, track context, and restrained blue-gray pencil before the final cleanup.

final_elements.name: central slotted rail
final_elements.description: A long foreshortened metal rail running diagonally beneath the blocks.

final_elements.name: two angled footplates
final_elements.description: A nearer left footplate and a farther right footplate tilted toward the runner.

final_elements.name: two hinge brackets
final_elements.description: Compact mounting brackets that visibly connect each footplate to the rail.

final_elements.name: rail slots and footplate grip texture
final_elements.description: Repeated rail adjustment slots plus a few directional grip lines on each plate.

final_elements.name: track lane edges and cast shadow
final_elements.description: Two cropped lane-edge strokes and a soft oval shadow anchoring the equipment to the track.

final_elements.name: blue-gray metal accents
final_elements.description: Restrained blue-gray colored-pencil accents on the rail and footplates.

frames.visible_job: Uses two pale converging guide lines and a center axis to establish the central slotted rail at a three-quarter angle.
Adds two clear wedge-and-rectangle footplate silhouettes at different positions along the rail while reserving space for their mounts.
Connects each existing footplate to the rail with one compact hinge bracket and clarifies the surviving outer contours.
Adds the repeated adjustment slots to the existing rail and a few directional grip lines inside both established footplates.
introduces: rail slots and footplate grip texture

frames.step_name: Ground the blocks on the track
frames.visible_job: Adds two cropped lane-edge strokes, a soft cast shadow, and light blue-gray pencil accents to the already-built equipment.
introduces: track lane edges and cast shadow
requires_prior_elements: rail slots and footplate grip texture

final_step.visible_job: Strengthens keeper contours, deepens existing blue-gray accents, and tidies the established track shadow without adding new structure.
allowed_changes: deeper blue-gray colored-pencil accents on existing metal shapes
restrained graphite shading inside established forms

rejection_checks: Both footplates must stay attached to the same central rail in every panel after they appear.
The two footplates may not swap sides, change angle, or drift in scale.
Rail slots, grip texture, lane edges, and blue-gray accents must appear before the finished panel.
No panel may render hidden rail detail underneath a hinge bracket or footplate mount.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must remain a believable handmade sketch rather than polished product art.
